content:
Character: Oliver Grant
Perception: Oliver is pleasant-looking in a wholesome, Midwestern way — the rolled sleeves, the messenger bag, the five-o'clock shadow of a man too busy studying to shave. He's not my world at all, which is exactly why talking to him at the coffee shop feels like a small vacation from my own life. He's clearly smart and disciplined — those CFA books are brutal and he's grinding through them alone, which I respect deeply. He's earnest in a way that makes me feel slightly fraudulent. I think he sees me as that influencer from upstairs, pleasant but alien. I see him as proof that ambition doesn't have to be performative. I know he's studying for the CFA. I helped him once by recommending a coffee order when he looked like he hadn't slept.
summary:

He's not my world at all, which is exactly why talking to him at the coffee shop feels like a small vacation from my own life. He's clearly smart and disciplined — those CFA books are brutal and he's grinding through them alone, which I respect deeply. He's earnest in a way that makes me feel slightly fraudulent. I think he sees me as that influencer from upstairs, pleasant but alien. I see him as proof that ambition doesn't have to be performative. I know he's studying for the CFA. I helped him once by recommending a coffee order when he looked like he hadn't slept.
